message:
fix(transport,channel,time): robust load balancer selection, broadcast lock safety, timer cancel cleanup

Three targeted fixes across the transport and channel subsystems:

1. LoadBalancer.select_n_random_small_without_materializing (renamed from
   select_n_random_all_healthy_small): The previous implementation
   required ALL endpoints to be healthy before using the fast Floyd
   sampling path, falling through to the slow materialize-and-shuffle
   path whenever any endpoint was unhealthy. The new implementation
   counts healthy endpoints, samples healthy-rank indices via Floyd's
   algorithm, then maps sampled ranks back to concrete endpoint
   references in a single forward scan. Returns None for the caller
   to fall back only when concurrent state changes invalidate the
   rank mapping mid-scan. The let-chain is replaced with a nested if
   for clarity.

2. broadcast.rs test fix: The lock-contention drop race assertions
   previously held the channel inner lock while asserting on fields
   inside it. Extracted total_sent and buffer_empty into local bindings
   before dropping the lock guard, preventing potential deadlock if
   assert_with_log! panics while the lock is held.

3. wheel.rs: TimerWheel::cancel now calls entry.remove() instead of
   entry.remove_entry(), since only the removal side effect is needed
   and the returned key is unused.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
